seo.title: Building Voice Agents with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build voice agents with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building voice agents with AutoGen enables real-time conversational AI through multi-agent orchestration, combining speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech for natural human interactions. However, the high-stakes nature of voice interfaces demands robust guardrails to prevent infinite loops, unsafe outputs, and excessive latency that can break user trust. Vex integration ensures reliability by filtering agent replies against strict latency, safety, and coherence criteria before audio synthesis.

architectureOverview.heading: Voice Agents Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
Voice agents with AutoGen follow a real-time pipeline: audio input is transcribed via speech-to-text (e.g., Whisper), fed into an AutoGen orchestrator managing specialized agents (listener, reasoner, responder), then synthesized back to speech (e.g., ElevenLabs). Agents collaborate in a conversational loop, but require sub-second end-to-end latency for natural flow. Vex intercepts agent replies as a filter function to enforce verification before TTS.

The AutoGen group chat manager coordinates agents, but voice constraints add latency budgets and safety checks. Common patterns use `register_reply` hooks to inject Vex verification, ensuring responses pass appropriateness, coherence, and safety before audio generation. Human-in-the-loop can override via voice commands.

Failure modes like infinite debates or unsafe code execution are amplified in voice due to real-time expectations and lack of visual feedback.

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents ping-pong without converging, causing latency violations and unnatural pauses in voice output
risks.mitigation: Vex conversation state tracking with loop detection and termination

risks.name: Code execution producing unsafe outputs
risks.description: Voice agents executing unverified code from user speech can generate harmful audio content
risks.mitigation: Vex sandboxed code execution verification before TTS synthesis

risks.name: Agents agreeing on incorrect conclusions
risks.description: Multi-agent consensus on factual errors leads to confidently wrong voice responses
risks.mitigation: Vex fact-checking and coherence scoring across agent replies

risks.name: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
risks.description: Extended agent debates exceed latency budgets, causing speech synthesis delays >500ms
risks.mitigation: Vex token budget enforcement with early termination signals

implementation.title: Basic AutoGen Voice Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize AutoGen agents with real-time voice pipeline integration
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "your-key"}]}
listener = AssistantAgent(name="Listener", llm_config=llm_config)
reasoner = AssistantAgent(name="Reasoner", llm_config=llm_config)
responder = AssistantAgent(name="Responder", llm_config=llm_config)

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[listener, reasoner, responder], messages=[])
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config=llm_config)

implementation.title: Speech-to-Text Integration
implementation.description: Real-time transcription feeding into AutoGen conversation
implementation.code:
import whisper
model = whisper.load_model("tiny.en")

def transcribe_audio(audio_bytes):
    result = model.transcribe(audio_bytes, fp16=False)
    return result["text"]

# Feed to AutoGen
transcript = transcribe_audio(audio_input)
listener.initiate_chat(responder, message=transcript)

implementation.title: Latency-Monitored Reply Handler
implementation.description: Wrap AutoGen replies with timing enforcement for voice latency
implementation.code:
import time
def voice_reply_handler(recipient, messages, sender, config):
    start_time = time.time()
    reply = recipient.generate_reply(messages, sender, config)
    latency = time.time() - start_time
    if latency > 0.4:  # 400ms budget
        reply = "[LATENCY_VIOLATION] Please repeat."
    return reply

implementation.title: Text-to-Speech Output
implementation.description: Convert verified AutoGen response to real-time speech
implementation.code:
from elevenlabs import generate, play

def synthesize_response(text):
    audio = generate(text=text, voice="Adam", model="eleven_multilingual_v2")
    play(audio)

# After Vex verification
synthesize_response(verified_agent_reply)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex as Reply Filter
vexIntegration.description: Hook Vex verification into AutoGen's reply mechanism for all agents
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from autogen import register_function

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

def vex_verify_reply(sender, recipient, messages):
    result = vex.verify({
        "prompt": messages[-1]["content"],
        "response": recipient.last_message()["content"],
        "checks": ["appropriateness", "latency_budget", "coherence", "safety"]
    })
    return result["verified_response"] if result["pass"] else "[BLOCKED]"

# Register as filter
register_function(
    vex_verify_reply,
    caller=reasoner,
    executor=responder,
    name="vex_guardrail",
    description="Verify response safety and latency"
)

cta.heading: Secure Your AutoGen Voice Agents
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install autogen vex-sdk` and register as reply filter to achieve production-grade voice agent reliability.
